proposal.verdict: VERDICT 096 (the +13 offset's twentieth row — P083 -> V096)
proposal.claim_priced: the forgiving-streak grace contract verbatim: 'any action within the grace window is safe — miss the ideal beat by up to G and your streak survives; you only ever lose the multiplier on a REAL miss (an action later than G)'
proposal.honest_scope: DESIGN head: the lane ships NO combo/streak/grace multiplier at the grounding HEAD; the sim transfers the STRUCTURE of the shipped shared-budget pace surface games/mining/core/energy.py onto a proposed combo-multiplier surface, and the design-transfer NULL prices that transfer

pinned_model.transition: break check evaluated BEFORE the budget update; break at the FIRST action where (a) B - l < 0 (budget cannot cover the lateness) OR (b) l > G (real miss); either way m -> 1 and the accumulated m-1 bonus is wiped; else the action is recognized: B <- min(Bmax, B - l + R*[l <= 0]) and m <- min(M, m + 1)
pinned_model.budget_boundary: strict: B - l < 0 (NOT <= 0) — the < boundary moves every break step by one if misread as <=; the model-semantics NULL axis prices exactly that
pinned_model.replenish_rule_baseline: R = 1 added ONLY on a clean action l <= 0; a within-grace late action 0 < l <= G spends l with no replenish
pinned_model.replenish_rule_repair_b: R' = 1 added on ANY within-grace action 0 < l <= G (in addition to the clean-action replenish); net within-grace budget change becomes -(l-1)
pinned_model.steady_family: constant lateness l in {0,1,2,3,4} — the folk belief's own case ('I'm always a little late'); a witness family, not a measure over all input sequences

anchors_F3.loss_equals: break_step - 1 == floor(B0/l); strictly DECREASING in l; max at l=1 (10 of M=25)
anchors_F3.closed_form_C1: steady sim break_step == floor(B0/l) + 1 for l in {1,2,3}
anchors_F3.closed_form_C2: repair-(b) sim break_step == floor((B0 - l)/(l - 1)) + 2 for l in {2,3}, and l=1 survives to the horizon

hand_worlds_F4.a: B0=10 is exhausted after 10 spends of l=1, so the l=1 streak breaks at step 11, not inf — APPROVE excluded by inspection
hand_worlds_F4.b: floor(10/3) + 1 = 4 — the l=3 cliff by inspection
hand_worlds_F4.c: at l=0 the clean-action replenish pins B at Bmax, so the budget never drains — the survive cell by inspection
hand_worlds_F4.d: repair (b) net change at l=1 is -1 + 1 = 0 — l=1 survives by inspection

arm_r.draw_grammar: per trace EXACTLY 3 rng.randint draws in registered order — lateness pattern l in [0,4], horizon cap in [5,50], salt in [1,1000] (drawn-and-logged, unused in classification); one random.Random per seed; NO statistical gate (sole gates: the 3N draw sentinel and exact reproducibility, pass probability 1 for a correct implementation)
arm_r.class_rule: run the pinned automaton with the drawn constant lateness l for up to the drawn horizon cap H actions; REAL-MISS if a real miss (l>G) break occurs at step <= H (l=4 breaks at step 1); SILENT-BREAK if a budget break (l in {1,2,3}) occurs at step <= H; SURVIVE otherwise (l=0 pins at cap; or a within-grace break_step beyond the drawn horizon)
arm_r.class_stream_digest_procedure: sha256 of the UTF-8 bytes of ''.join(the FIRST character of each trace's class label in trace order — SURVIVE->'S', SILENT-BREAK->'S', REAL-MISS->'R'), hexdigest truncated to the first 12 chars

decision_rule.order: REJECT -> INVALID -> APPROVE -> NULL, REJECT evaluated FIRST
decision_rule.REJECT: R1 (the folk belief made precise: the census over l in {0,1,2,3} lands {l=0 survives; l=1,2,3 break at 11,6,4} — NOT 'all within-grace survive' — with the l>G control breaking at step 1) AND R2 (steady sim break_step == floor(B0/l)+1 exact across the pinned grid — C1) AND R3 (the forgiveness inversion: silent loss = break_step-1 = floor(B0/l) = {10,5,3} strictly DECREASING in l, max at l=1) AND R4 (repair (a) fires once per streak at break_step-1 with zero false positives on the l=0 cohort; repair (b) at R'=1 makes l=1 survive and moves l=2 to step 10, l=3 to step 5, each == its closed form — C2)
decision_rule.INVALID: any F1-F5 gate failure (controls misbehaving — report, no ruling)
decision_rule.APPROVE: every steady within-grace pattern l in {1,2,3} survives to the horizon — arithmetically excluded by the l=1 cell (B0=10 exhausted after 10 unit spends, break at 11); mutually exclusive with REJECT
decision_rule.NULL: anything else; named axes: model-semantics mismatch (the < 0 boundary / break-before-update order / replenish-only-on-clean / checked-first miss misread — the corrected law + re-derived census is the finding), constant-convention (a defensible alternative B0/G/R tuple that flips a clause ships the corrected tuple), design-transfer (the transfer of the energy-budget STRUCTURE to a multiplier the lane has not built judged unsound as a model of any plausible combo mechanic — a finalized NULL, never a re-run request)
decision_rule.gate_power: the sim is FULLY DETERMINISTIC in every decision leg; the only seeded arm is reporting-only with NO statistical gate; JOINT pass probability across all gates for a correct implementation = 1 EXACTLY

gates.F1: model identities: break check evaluated BEFORE the budget update on every step; B <= Bmax invariant holds; the multiplier reset wipes exactly m-1; every steady trace deterministic (two runs identical); the Arm-R draw counter == 3N exactly
gates.F2: structure results, exact: (a) the steady census over l in {0,1,2,3,4} at the registered break steps; (b) the loss map and its strict monotonicity; (c) repair-(a) once-per-streak at break_step-1 + the l=0 zero-false-positive; (d) repair-(b) break steps sim == closed form
gates.F3: census anchors (reference values, exact): the break-step map, the loss map, the repair maps, and the Arm-R previews/digests, verbatim from the Pinned model block
gates.F4: the hand worlds (pencil): (a) B0=10 exhausted after 10 spends of 1 -> break at 11; (b) floor(10/3)+1=4; (c) l=0 cap-pin survivor; (d) repair (b) net-0 at l=1
gates.F5: degeneracy/convention controls: the l=0 survivor and the l>G immediate real-miss both present; repair (a) fires zero times on the survivor cohort; repair (b) strictly EXTENDS survival vs baseline (never shortens); the Arm-R taxonomy is total on all N traces (SURVIVE + SILENT-BREAK + REAL-MISS == N); stdout + results byte-identical across two process runs (Arm R pinned to a stated CPython minor)

typed_contacts.C1: Arm A (stepwise automaton) steady break_step == the closed form floor(B0/l)+1 for l in {1,2,3}
typed_contacts.C2: Arm A repair-(b) break_step == the closed form floor((B0-l)/(l-1))+2 for l in {2,3}, l=1 survives
typed_contacts.twin_machine: Arm B (an INDEPENDENTLY-shaped automaton: a full-trajectory budget walk over an explicit horizon list, no early-return closed form) == Arm A on every steady pattern and every Arm-R trace
typed_contacts.twin_evaluators: two independently-written decision evaluators (an if-chain and a table-driven scorer) emit the same ruling token over an ENUMERATED boolean input set

vacancy_derived_disclosures.note: sim-chosen realizations of shape-registered slots — disclosed here BEFORE the runner, never match claims; every REGISTERED value above (the break-step map, the loss map, both repair maps, both closed forms, both Arm-R censuses AND both class-stream digests) is a match claim and must reproduce exactly
vacancy_derived_disclosures.decision_horizon: the decision-arm steady walks run to a fixed horizon of 1000 actions (safely beyond every finite break step 11/6/4 and every repair-(b) step 10; a non-breaking walk to the horizon is reported as SURVIVE/inf)
vacancy_derived_disclosures.class_stream_digest_procedure: the digest concatenates the FIRST character of each class label (SURVIVE/SILENT-BREAK both -> 'S', REAL-MISS -> 'R') — a survive-vs-real-miss position stream; the SURVIVE vs SILENT-BREAK split is asserted SEPARATELY and exactly by the per-seed census counts, so the digest and the census are independent witnesses
vacancy_derived_disclosures.salt_logging: the per-trace salt draw is logged in aggregate (sum + first 5 salts per seed) in results.json; the salt does not enter classification or the class stream
vacancy_derived_disclosures.presentation_target: seed 20261728 shuffles the display order of the steady-census table in stdout (presentation leg only)
